title: astragalus phrygius
intro: Astragalus phrygius, also known as Phrygia milk-vetch, is a perennial plant in the legume family native to Asia and Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, believed to help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including immune system support, anti-inflammatory effects, and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against diseases and promote longevity. Astragalus phrygius is often prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, or capsules for use in traditional medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
